channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: A whistleblower’s disclosure details how DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data
item.description: A whistleblower tells Congress and NPR that DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data and hid its tracks. “None of that information should ever leave the agency,” said a former NLRB official.
item.dc:creator: Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: First Mexico-born rep targets indictment-plagued Democrat in House GOP comeback bid
item.description: Former Rep. Mayra Flores is launching a comeback bid, this time eyeing Rep. Henry Cuellar's seat in South Texas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Medicaid puzzle confronts Minnesota lawmakers. Federal cuts could hit health safety net program
item.description: Republicans in Washington are working on slashing the federal budget to help pay for a slate of tax breaks. State lawmakers know it could mean a health care funding hole, but they don’t know how much they’ll have to backfill.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: US moves ahead on tariffs with investigations into computer chips and pharmaceuticals
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The Trump administration has taken its next steps toward imposing more tariffs on key imports, launching investigations into imports of computer chips, chip making equipment and pharmaceuticals.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Joe Biden will speak about Social Security in his return to national stage
item.description: Former President Joe Biden is returning to the national stage to help elevate concerns that President Donald Trump’s agenda is threatening the health of Social Security.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples And Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Core Democratic groups are preparing to be targeted by the Trump administration
item.description: Some of the Democratic Party’s core political groups are preparing for the possibility that President Donald Trump’s allies may soon launch criminal investigations that could handicap Democratic infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bill O'Reilly believes Trump has several tariff deals already done
item.description: Bill O'Reilly recently spoke with President Trump and believes the president has several tariff deals already done.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: White House proposes drastic cuts to State Department and funding for UN, NATO and other groups
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House’s Office of Management and Budget has proposed gutting the State Department’s budget by almost 50%, closing a number of overseas diplomatic missions, slashing the number of diplomatic staff, and eliminating funding for nearly all international organizations, includi
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Another pro-Palestine Columbia protestor detained by ICE, Sanders and Dems outraged
item.description: Another pro-Palestine Columbia student protester was detained by federal immigration authorities amid the Trump administration’s crackdown on alleged threats to national security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOT saves taxpayers over $60M by terminating Texas high-speed rail contract
item.description: Department of Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy announced Monday that $60 million-plus grant to develop a high-speed rail corridor in Texas has been terminated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he wants to imprison US citizens in El Salvador. That's likely illegal
item.description: President Donald Trump keeps talking about sending U.S. citizens who commit violent crimes to prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DOGE uncovers massive VA contract for ‘salary survey data and analysis'
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) on Monday announced more cuts within the Department of Veterans Affairs by millions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance fumbles Ohio State's NCAA football championship trophy as Buckeyes visit the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President JD Vance ended the Ohio State football team's visit to the White House on Monday by fumbling the team's national championship trophy.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Whitmer explains her Oval Office folder fiasco
item.description: Gov. Gretchen Whitmer, D-Mich., explained she didn't want her photo taken in the Oval Office when she held a folder in front of her face in the now-viral moment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom signs $2.8B bailout for healthcare program overrun by illegal immigrants
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom signed off on a $2.8B loan to cover healthcare program shortfall due to underestimated costs of having illegal immigrants covered.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voters widely oppose taxpayer-funded gender surgeries, revealing Democrat Party's vulnerability: poll
item.description: Taxpayer-funded transgender treatments remain widely unpopular among American voters, according to a new poll done by American Principles Project.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rep Ro Khanna tees up 2028 rivalry during pointed speech in JD Vance's home state
item.description: Considered a 2028 presidential contender, Rep. Ro Khanna delivered remarks in Vice President JD Vance's home state of Ohio, framing an early rivalry between the two rising political leaders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump sued over 'Liberation Day' tariffs
item.description: President Trump’s “Liberation Day” tariffs came under their first major legal challenge Monday, brought by a libertarian public-interest firm that argues the president overstepped his authority.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Despite a court order, White House bars AP from Oval Office event
item.description: The Trump administration has turned a reporter and photographer from The Associated Press away from covering an Oval Office news conference involving President Donald Trump and El Salvador President Nayib Bukele.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrats dislike the ‘chaos’ of Trump’s trade war but are OK with some tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats are quick to say that President Donald Trump’s tariffs are horrible, awful, terrible. But Democrats are also stressing that they are not inherently anti-tariff. What Trump's political opponents say they really dislike is the “chaos” he has unleashed.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sanders jokingly calls Ocasio-Cortez his daughter at Utah rally
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) joked during a joint appearance at a Utah rally on Sunday that Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) was his daughter, praising the progressive lawmaker and highlighting her background.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Both the US and El Salvador refuse to return wrongly deported man to the US
item.description: President Donald Trump’s top advisers and Nayib Bukele, the president of El Salvador, said Monday that they had no basis for the small Central American nation to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland man who was wrongly deported there last month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump hosts El Salvador's Nayib Bukele amid deportation controversy
item.description: Since March, El Salvador has accepted more than 200 Venezuelan immigrants accused by the U.S. of gang activity and violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Plans to celebrate America's 250th anniversary were underway. Then came the federal funding cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Community celebrations being planned to commemorate the nation's 250th anniversary next year are at risk of being significantly scaled back or canceled because of federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump's administration, according to multiple state humanities councils acr
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Africa appoints a special US envoy after its ambassador was expelled
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South African President Cyril Ramaphosa appointed a former deputy finance minister as a special envoy to the United States on Monday after the ambassador was expelled last month by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro and the alleged arson at his official residence
item.description: A man is facing charges after authorities say he broke into the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion in the middle of the night, set a fire that left significant damage and forced Gov. Josh Shapiro, his family and guests to evacuate the building during the Jewish holiday of Passover.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump is 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump’s doctor says the oldest man elected president is “fully fit” to serve as commander in chief as the White House released results Sunday of Trump's recent physical exam.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bill de Blasio on what's next for the Democratic Party
item.description: De Blasio, who has received fierce criticism from President Trump this week, believes the Democrats blew the presidential election on multiple fronts.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Will the SAVE Act make it harder for married women to vote? We ask legal experts
item.description: Legal experts say states could help married women who have changed their last names by accepting documents like a legal decree or a marriage certificate, but it might not fix the issue for all.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariffs bring Democrat disagreements to surface
item.description: President Donald Trump’s tariffs are putting Democrats in a challenging political position as they decide how strongly to denounce his trade policy.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans fear Trump’s trade war could lead to political wipeout
item.description: Republican lawmakers say there’s a good chance that President Trump's trade war will boomerang on Republicans politically in 2026, as rising prices and shrinking growth could offset other accomplishments by the GOP.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Judge relaxes ban on DOGE access to sensitive US Treasury information
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York judge has relaxed a ban she’d put on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency preventing it from accessing sensitive Treasury Department information related to millions of Americans. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration ordered to unfreeze funding in dispute with Maine over transgender students
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to unfreeze funds intended for a Maine child nutrition program that were suspended amid a disagreement between the state and the president over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: GOP frustrations with conservatives rise after budget fight
item.description: Frustrations are bubbling up among House Republicans after the conference — by the skin of its teeth — overcame internal disputes to adopt a framework for President Trump’s legislative agenda, a troubling sign for the group as it heads into the next, more difficult, step in achieving the president’s
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Bondi appoints new interim U.S. Attorney for San Diego, Imperial counties
item.description: The Southern District of California will have a new U.S attorney after Attorney General Pam Bondi appointed a longtime San Diego prosecutor to take over the role as the region's top federal law enforcement official following Tara McGrath's termination.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces $600 million in new deals with five law firms
item.description: The deals bring to nine the number of firms that have struck agreements with Trump in the wake of numerous actions targeting major law offices.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Stocks slide as Trump's trade war with China spooks Wall Street
item.description: Stocks slid Thursday as President Trump’s escalating trade war with China and concerns about its economic implications continued to rattle Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WFLA, NBC
